message:
feat(runtime): add thread-local RuntimeHandle discovery via block_on (issue #21)

Enable futures running inside Runtime::block_on() to discover the
current runtime and spawn tasks onto the real scheduler without
threading the handle through every layer.

Implementation:
- Add CURRENT_RUNTIME_HANDLE thread-local with RefCell<Option<RuntimeHandle>>
- Add ScopedRuntimeHandle RAII guard that installs/restores the
  thread-local on construction/drop, supporting nested block_on calls
- Runtime::block_on() installs the guard before polling the future
- Runtime::current_handle() reads the thread-local, returning
  Option<RuntimeHandle>

This solves issue #21 by allowing library code deep in the call stack
to spawn tasks without requiring explicit handle plumbing.

Add four tests:
- current_handle_available_inside_block_on: Some inside block_on
- current_handle_none_outside_block_on: None outside any runtime
- current_handle_spawn_completes_on_scheduler: spawned task runs and
  returns a value through the discovered handle
- current_handle_restored_after_block_on: thread-local properly
  restored to None after block_on returns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
